steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage states “evaluations varied widely depending on which occupation was being considered.” The example must compare different occupations to demonstrate this variation.

steps.title: Match the contrast across occupations
steps.content:
Choice C presents tour guide at 82% likely versus surgeon at only 16% likely—a dramatic difference between occupations that directly illustrates the variation.

Choices A and D provide single-occupation data without comparison, while B highlights matching percentages rather than variation.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
